rulings.Case Title: DEAN BALLBACK et al VS. THOMAS H ROBERTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 23, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANTS THOMAS ROBERTS, LON MURPHY, MASON-MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE, INC. FKA PRUDENTIAL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: DEAN BALLBACK et al VS. THOMAS H ROBERTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 23, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANTS THOMAS ROBERTS, LON MURPHY, MASON-MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE, INC. FKA PRUDENTIAL DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (Oral Breach of K) is SUSTAINED With leave to amend for failure to state facts showing breach. The Court previously granted P Leave to amend to plead the terms of the K and breach more clearly. P has not done so. FAC 24 states that Roberts breached the oral K by failing to properly manage the affairs of the LLC and the construction of the property by misappropriating and/or squandering the LLC's funds. P never alleged that such management was actually a covenant of the alleged oral K. The demurrer to the 1st cause of action on the grounds that the oral K is superseded by the Listing Agreement or Operating Agreement is OVERRULED. The Court DENIES D's RJN of the Listing Agreement. Furthermore, the Court is bound to accept Ps' interpretation of the Listing Agreement and Operating Agreement as true for matters of a demurrer. See Aragon Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239. Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (Negligent Misrep) is OVERRULED. P has stated a cause of action with the requisite particularity. Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (Breach of Fiduciary Duty) is OVERRULED. Ps have stated that Roberts was acting in his capacity as a real estate broker, which gives rise to a fiduciary relationship. Demurrer to the 4th cause of action is SUSTAINED with Leave to amend for the same reasons as the 1st cause of action. Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is OVERRULED. An accounting is necessary to determine the amount of funds allegedly misappropriated that are not accounted for on the financial records provided to Ps in 3/08. Demurrer on SOL grounds is SUSTAINED With Leave to amend. P must specifically plead facts showing: (1) the time and manner of discovery; and (2) facts that show P's inability to have earlier discove

rulings.Case Title: HENRY R. FISHER VS. MOHAMMED R. AHMADI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order To Transfer Case To Los Angeles
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 23, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANTS MOHAMMED AHMAD, IRENE AHMADI'S Motion For Order To Transfer Case To Los Angeles. DENIED. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT VENUE IS IMPROPER. THE LOAN WAS FOR INVESTMENT PURPOSES IN DEVELOPING A PLOT OF LAND, NOT PRIMARILY FOR PERSONAL, FAMILY, OR HOUSEHOLD USE. THE LAST ACT NECESSARY FOR THE CONTRACT TO BE FORMED OCCURRED IN SAN FRANCISCO. (CCP SECTION 395(a)) THE PLACE OF PERFORMANCE WAS SPECIALLY SET FORTH IN THE PROMISSORY NOTE AS SAN FRANCISCO AND THE MODIFICATION DID NOT AMEND THAT PROVISION. THE ARGUMENT TO TRANSFER BASED ON CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS PREMATURE. (SEE EASTON V. S.C. (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE ACQUISITION, LLC, A VS. DAN LU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff San Francisco Office Acquisition, Llc, Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 23, 2011, line 7, Plaintiff San Francisco Office Acquisition, LLC, Motion For Summary Judgment. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHIFTS THE BURDEN AND DEFENDANTS RAISE NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. THE LICENSE AGREEMENT IS ACTUALLY A SUBLEASE AS A MATTER OF LAW. THE AGREEMENT REQUIRES TSAN TO PAY LU A SET AMOUNT THAT INCLUDES RENT, PLUS PROPERTY TAX, BUILDING INSURANCE, TAXES, UTILITIES AND OTHER OPERATING EXPENSES, ALL RAW MATERIALS AND SUPPLIES, AND A SECURITY DEPOSIT FOR A SET TERM OF 2 YEARS. PREVIOUSLY, A SIMILAR DOCUMENT WAS USED BY DEFENDANTS ENTITLED AS "BUSINESS LEASE AGREEMENT" BUT THE TITLE WAS MODIFIED. (EX. 5; LU DEPO 87:23-88:19) THE LICENSE AGREEMENT CONFERS THE WHOLE BUSINESS INTEREST TO TSAN WHO WAS GIVEN THE EXCLUSIVE RIGHT TO SELL FOOD AND BEVERAGES (LU DEPO 94:4-8) LU ADMITTED TO PARAS THAT TSAN WAS LEASING THE BUSINESS FROM HIM. (PARAS DEPO. 24:14-16) TSAN WAS REQUIRED TO PAY ALL EXPENSES AND WAS ENTITLED TO KEEP ALL THE INCOME FROM THE OPERATION OF THE BUSINESS. DEFENDANTS ARE IN VIOLATION OF LEASE ART. XVI IN THAT THEY TRANSFERRED, ASSIGNED OR SUBLET WITHOUT FIRST OBTAINING OWNER'S WRITTEN CONSENT. DEFENDANTS RAISE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. DEFENDANTS CONSISTENTLY DENIED THAT THEY WERE SUBLEASING THE PREMISES AND PLAINTIFF HAD REMINDED DEFENDANTS THAT THEY COULD NOT SUBLEASE WITHOUT CONSENT. (EX. 4) PLAINTIFFS WERE NOT ESTOPPED FROM REFUSING TO ALLOW THE OPTION TO BE EXERCISED IN VIEW OF DEFENDANTS DEFAULT. NO UNCLEAN HANDS OR WAIVER IS DEMONSTRATED. SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED FOR POSSESSION OF THE PREMISES. =(302/CWW)
